customTitle: Why is parquet smaller than I expect?

message.content: I wrote a 10M-row dataframe to parquet and got a 12MB file. Is that right? Csv would be ~3GB.

content.text: Yes, that's normal and expected. Parquet stores columnar data with per-column dictionary encoding, RLE for runs of identical values, and either snappy or zstd compression on top. If your data has any structure (repeated categories, monotonic timestamps, integer ranges with small dictionaries) the compression ratio can hit 100-300×. A sanity check: run `pyarrow.parquet.ParquetFile(path).schema_arrow` and `.metadata` — you'll see per-column compressed and uncompressed sizes broken out by row group.
